company.name: Nektar Therapeutics
company.subtitle: Post-Phase 2b AD/AA · Phase 3 ZENITH-AD starting Q2 2026 · BLA target 2029

market.notes: AD market ~$15-20B (Dupixent alone >$13B). REZOLVE-AD Phase 2b (393pts): primary met all doses (p<0.001). Maintenance (Feb 2026): durable Q4W and Q12W, EASI-100 18-22% at wk52, deepening response. No conjunctivitis/oral herpes signal vs JAKi. BLA target 2029. Competitors: Dupixent, Rinvoq, Cibinqo, Adbry, Ebglyss.

us.patientsK.note: NEA: ~1.5M US moderate-severe AD on systemic therapy; ~16.5M US adults have AD symptoms total.

eu.patientsK.note: ETFAD: ~2.5M EU27+UK moderate-severe AD systemic-eligible; EASI/IGA severity scoring.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~20M mod-severe; Japan high prevalence + strong Dupixent uptake; China rapid growth.

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- Dupixent commercial/Medicare Part D; Rinvoq/Cibinqo JAKi; Adbry (tralokinumab); Ebglyss (lebrikizumab) new.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~48% -- NICE/G-BA cover Dupixent + JAKi restricted use (BBW); Adbry/Ebglyss rolling out.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- Japan J-DPS + China NRDL added Dupixent; cyclosporine generic + steroids baseline elsewhere.

us.priceK.note: Blended $38K: Dupixent $40K + Rinvoq $65K + Cibinqo $60K + Adbry $40K + Ebglyss $40K; cyclosporine generic $3K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$22K blended -- Dupixent net ~$25K + JAKi ~$30K; cyclosporine/MTX generic backbone.

row.priceK.note: ~$8K -- cyclosporine generic + phototherapy; biologics <15% penetration ex-Japan.

penPct.note: ~35% peak -- rapid biologic/JAKi expansion; OX40 (rocatinlimab, amlitelimab) pipeline adding options.

us.reachPct.note: Rezpegaldesleukin (IL-2) P2b AD positive 2024, P3 launching 2025; 3% of ~16M US mod-sev AD if approved 2027 vs Dupixent/Rinvoq dominant.

us.priceK.note: $32K/yr WAC modeled (q2wk SC); Dupixent parity ($37K) discount.

us.wtpPct.note: 35% -- payors require Dupixent step-edit; IL-2 Treg MOA differentiates but efficacy ~EASI-50.

market.notes: AA market ~$3-5B. High-dose: 28.2% SALT reduction vs 11.2% placebo (36wk). Stat sig achieved with exclusions. Competitors: Olumiant, Litfulo (both JAKi with safety concerns). Treg MOA potentially safer long-term.

us.patientsK.note: National Alopecia Areata Foundation: ~300K US AA active; ~700K lifetime risk; ~20% severe (AT/AU).

eu.patientsK.note: EADV: ~450K EU27+UK AA active; SALT severity scale; autoimmune comorbidity common.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~4M AA; Japan JAK inhibitor early adopter; China limited novel access.

us.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- Olumiant (baricitinib, 2022) + Litfulo (ritlecitinib, 2023) Medicare/commercial; intralesional steroids off-label.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~32% -- NICE/G-BA approved Olumiant/Litfulo severe AA; topical/intralesional steroids standard baseline.

row.wtpPct.note: ~10% -- Japan approved baricitinib/ritlecitinib; topical minoxidil + steroids elsewhere.

us.priceK.note: Blended $42K: Olumiant $30K + Litfulo $50K (severe AA SALT>=50); topical minoxidil + steroids $500 baseline.

eu.priceK.note: ~$22K blended -- Olumiant net ~$18K + Litfulo ~$30K; restricted to severe.

row.priceK.note: ~$4K -- minoxidil + steroids + limited JAKi access in Japan.

penPct.note: ~20% peak -- JAKi class expanding (Rinvoq, deuruxolitinib pipeline); severe AA first, moderate expansion next.

us.reachPct.note: Rezpeg alopecia areata P2b readout 2025; 5% of ~700K US severe AA if approved 2028 vs Litfulo/Olumiant.

us.priceK.note: $40K/yr WAC modeled; Litfulo $50K, Olumiant $30K; positioning premium for safety.

us.wtpPct.note: 45% -- payors require SALT>=50 + JAKi contraindication or failure; IL-2 safety advantage vs JAK.

market.notes: Early stage. Treg restoration could preserve beta-cell function. Large unmet need but uncertain regulatory path. Option value.

us.patientsK.note: CDC 2023 + JDRF: ~1.9M US T1D (adults + pediatric); ~200K newly diagnosed annually, auto-antibody positive.

eu.patientsK.note: IDF Atlas 2023: ~1.5M EU5+UK T1D; Nordic/Finland highest incidence globally (60/100K).

row.patientsK.note: IDF global: ~8M T1D ex-US/EU (India, China rising; historically under-diagnosed).

us.wtpPct.note: ~85% -- Medicare, Medicaid, commercial cover insulin + CGM; Inflation Reduction Act $35/mo insulin cap.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~80% -- Universal coverage for insulin analogs, CGM reimbursed in UK/Germany/France for T1D.

row.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- Japan/AU covered; emerging markets basal-bolus access limited, CGM mostly self-pay.

us.priceK.note: $5K/yr blended: insulin ($1-3K post-IRA cap), CGM Dexcom/Libre ($2-3K), pump $4-6K amortized; Lantidra cell therapy $500K one-time.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$2.5K: insulin generics ~$500, CGM ~$1.5K reimbursed tier.

row.priceK.note: $1K blended: biosimilar insulin dominant, CGM penetration growing in China/Japan.

penPct.note: ~12% peak -- Tzield (teplizumab) delays onset in Stage 2; Vertex VX-880 islet cell pipeline limited near-term.

us.reachPct.note: Rezpeg T1D preservation P2 2025; 2% of ~2M US T1D + new-onset window if approved 2029; Tzield first-to-market prevention.

us.priceK.note: $190K/course WAC modeled (Tzield $194K precedent); one-time prevention therapy.

us.wtpPct.note: 40% -- payors require C-peptide + GAD/IA-2 antibody; new-onset <100d window like Tzield.

market.notes: IL-15 agonist to enhance cancer immunotherapy. Combo trials with Bavencio, Imfinzi, Breyanzi, TIL. Phase 2/3 with CAR-T in LBCL ongoing. Non-core but optionality.

us.patientsK.note: ~5K US CAR-T eligible R/R CLL/NHL per yr; limited by fitness + center access. Source: CIBMTR, Kite/Gilead Q reports.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~4K CAR-T infused/yr; EBMT CAR-T registry; capacity-constrained.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~3K; Japan + China rapid build-out of CAR-T centers 2023-25.

us.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- Breyanzi/Yescarta R/R DLBCL + CLL; Medicare transitional pass-through; center-based billing.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~38% -- NICE via CDF; G-BA Erstattungsbetrag negotiated; EBMT capacity bottleneck.

row.wtpPct.note: ~10% -- Japan MHLW covers; China domestic CAR-T (relma-cel, cilta-cel) lower priced.

us.priceK.note: Blended $440K: Breyanzi $470K (40% pts) + Yescarta $460K (35%) + in-house CAR-T $350K (15%) + combo w/bispecific $520K (10%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$260K blended; EU net ~60% US list post negotiation.

row.priceK.note: ~$85K blended; China domestic CAR-T ~$75K drives mix.

penPct.note: ~30% peak -- capacity + fitness gate; allo/off-the-shelf CAR-T + CAR-T+bispecific combos expand access 5yr.

us.reachPct.note: NKTR-255 IL-15 agonist P2/3 combo post-CAR-T LBCL 2025; 5% of ~10K US post-CAR-T relapsed if approved 2028.

us.priceK.note: $120K/course WAC modeled (6 infusions); combo with standard CAR-T cost stack.

us.wtpPct.note: 55% -- payors cover per NCCN post-CAR-T relapse; narrow academic-center footprint.

mega_bear.narrative: ZENITH-AD Phase 3 fails — Treg approach dead — another Nektar reinvention
events: ✗ ZENITH-AD Phase 3 misses primary endpoint
✗ Treg stimulation doesn't translate from Phase 2 to Phase 3
✗ AA and T1D programs shelved — pipeline collapse
→ $720M cash (~$25/share) is the stock, market assigns zero pipeline value

bear.narrative: Phase 3 disappoints or efficacy gap vs Dupixent too wide
events: ✗ ZENITH-AD Phase 3 fails to replicate Phase 2b
✗ Efficacy gap vs Dupixent too wide for market adoption
✗ AA data insufficient for pivotal
→ Re-rates to cash + residual pipeline

base.narrative: AD Phase 3 confirms — safety + quarterly dosing differentiate
events: ✓ ZENITH-AD replicates Phase 2b efficacy
✓ Q12W maintenance differentiates vs Dupixent Q2W
~ AA advances to Phase 3
→ BLA 2029, launch 2030 as best-in-class Treg

bull.narrative: Treg platform validated across derm + autoimmune — M&A
events: ✓ AD Phase 3 best-in-class safety + competitive efficacy
✓ Quarterly dosing wins formulary preference
✓ AA Phase 3 — Treg platform validated
✓ M&A target for big pharma

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Treg platform creates new therapeutic class — AD + AA + T1D + lupus — Pfizer acquires
events: ✓ ZENITH-AD best-in-class: quarterly dosing + disease modification
✓ AA Phase 3 positive — first Treg therapy approved in derm
✓ T1D Phase 2 shows beta-cell preservation — disease-modifying
✓ Platform extends to lupus, MS — Pfizer acquires for Treg franchise at $150+

lastData.title: REZOLVE-AD maintenance + AAD 2026 presentations
points: 36-week maintenance: durable with monthly AND quarterly dosing
EASI-100 (complete clearance): 18-22% at week 52, deepening over time
No conjunctivitis, oral herpes, malignancy (vs JAKi safety edge)
AAD: consistent efficacy across moderate + severe (vIGA 3 and 4)
AA PoC: 28.2% vs 11.2% SALT reduction (high dose, 36wk)
Phase 3 ZENITH-AD planned Q2 2026, BLA target 2029

nextCatalyst.title: Phase 3 ZENITH-AD initiation (mod-severe AD)
thresholds: Phase 3 design and primary endpoint disclosure
Dose confirmed from Phase 2b (24µg/kg Q2W → Q4W/Q12W maint.)
Also: T1D Phase 2 data, NKTR-255 CAR-T combo

risks: Phase 3 still 3+ years from BLA — long execution timeline
Dupixent dominates ($13B+) — must show competitive efficacy
1000% rally — massive pullback risk on any stumble
Phase 2b→3 translation risk (393pts may not predict pivotal)
SC injection — oral JAKi may be preferred despite safety edge
AA stat sig required exclusions — signal weaker than headline
No pipeline revenue — legacy royalties $55M/yr declining

catalysts.title: REZOLVE-AA 52-week alopecia areata data
catalysts._rationale: Management confirmed 52-week AA data expected in April 2026
